principle: Update the policy along a rotation between the principal eigendirection of the empirical Fisher information and the REINFORCE score-function gradient, parameterized by a single angular knob, so the trajectory's own policy-variation direction becomes a load-bearing primitive that is non-zero at random init and gradually aligns with reward-driven gradient as training proceeds.
primitive_type: rollout tau -> unit vector in policy-parameter tangent space (top eigenvector of empirical Fisher F_tau = sum_t g_t g_t^T)
feedback_signal: scalar environment reward (sparse-stage envs MiniGrid-DoorKey-8x8-v0 and MiniGrid-KeyCorridorS3R3-v0 are natively scalar; no info['vector'] reduction); the Fisher principal direction is reward-free and computed purely from per-step policy scores
novelty_boundary: GRADCOMP rotates the update toward the top Fisher eigenvector with a gradient-magnitude-annealed angle and a non-zero floor-magnitude that fires at cold start; NPG amplifies low-Fisher directions via inversion (opposite spectral end) and produces zero update when the gradient is zero; TRPO/PPO impose KL trust regions on a REINFORCE direction; ES uses random Gaussian perturbations rather than a trajectory-informed eigenvector; v1 is not a curiosity bonus (does not modify reward) and not a trajectory-weight variant (modifies update direction, not weight). Sparse-reward MiniGrid envs are the natural test bed because REINFORCE has g_tau = 0 until first reward, exactly the cold-phase regime GRADCOMP is designed to escape.
empirical_claim: On MiniGrid-DoorKey-8x8-v0 and MiniGrid-KeyCorridorS3R3-v0 (sparse stage, scalar reward, 120s budget), GRADCOMP should reach the first non-zero-return episode strictly earlier than REINFORCE in median across seeds AND match or exceed REINFORCE on mean episodic return, while showing (i) larger parameter updates than REINFORCE during the pre-first-reward cold phase and (ii) v1 . g_hat alignment drifting from ~0 toward >=0.3 as training progresses post-first-reward.
falsifier: If GRADCOMP's median first-reward-episode index on DoorKey-8x8 is identical within seed variance to REINFORCE, OR the alignment v1.g_hat stays near zero throughout training, OR GRADCOMP's mean episodic return on KeyCorridorS3R3 is strictly worse than REINFORCE, the Fisher rotation primitive is not load-bearing in its design regime. If the random-unit-vector ablation matches GRADCOMP on these observables, the trajectory-informed nature of v1 is not load-bearing.
ablation_plan: Replace v1(tau) with a uniformly-random unit vector xi ~ Uniform(S^{n-1}) resampled per rollout; preserve the slerp interpolation, the eta annealer, the gradient floor c, and all hyperparameters. The ablation walks along a random direction in parameter space at cold start and reduces to REINFORCE at warm start, identical to GRADCOMP's warm behavior. Discriminating observables: alignment v1.g_hat (should be near 0 for random ablation, growing positive for GRADCOMP) and median episode index of first non-zero return on MiniGrid-DoorKey-8x8-v0 (should be earlier for GRADCOMP if v1 carries useful structure). A second sanity ablation pins eta = 1 always (pure REINFORCE, no compass) to isolate whether the Fisher rotation contributes anything beyond the gradient floor.
proof_debt: (1) Convergence under annealed eta_t schedule with Robbins-Monro step sizes -- show that the cold-phase bias is bounded and absorbed by warm-phase REINFORCE convergence. (2) Effective-rank monotonicity claim: along the cold-phase iterate, the expected effective rank of E_tau[F_tau] is monotonically non-decreasing -- this is the load-bearing structural claim that v1-walking increases policy expressivity without reward. (3) At convergence, v1(tau) -> g_hat_tau (alignment limit), so GRADCOMP and REINFORCE share fixed points. (4) Variance bound on the Gram-trick top-eigenvector estimator and its propagation to the parameter update. (5) First-reward-time bound: on a deterministic sparse-reward MDP, bound the expected first-non-zero-return episode index for GRADCOMP relative to REINFORCE.
